description: Launch-gate umbrella closing the six blockers in the 2026-06-08 launch-readiness report (verdict: NOT READY). Pricing-page and installer blockers are cross-referenced to existing F-003/F-004 stories rather than duplicated; new stories cover the uncovered cross-cutting work (API test-suite health, Next.js advisory, Rust/CI verification) plus the owner-gated signup-CTA repair. Each story is tagged agent-buildable vs owner/operator/environment-gated. Under probation (trust 0), only agent-buildable stories execute without explicit owner go-ahead.

governance.note: Build/deploy/auth/database/financial stories are gated. Planning is non-destructive and permitted. Owner-action items verify via the F-003 evidence-file pattern (evidence/launch-readiness/).

executorLegend.agent: Agent can execute now under probation (non-destructive, test-only, or artifact-drafting).
executorLegend.owner: Requires owner decision/approval — touches auth, pricing, or irreversible surface.
executorLegend.environment: Blocked on local/CI toolchain that is not configured.

userStories.title: Triage API test-suite failures into fix-categories
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the 25 failures + 31 errors categorized into {test-only fixture, stale-expectation, real-bug-protected} so that the buildable subset is separated from owner-gated database/auth fixes before any code is touched.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/launch-readiness/US-001-pytest-triage.md exists with verbatim `python3 -m pytest api/tests -q` tail
Every failure/error is assigned to exactly one category with the file:test id and the chosen category
`grep -c 'category:' evidence/launch-readiness/US-001-pytest-triage.md` >= 56 (one per failure+error)
Real-bug-protected items name the production module they would touch (e.g. api/database.py)

userStories.title: Fix event-loop fixture errors in API test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the `got Future attached to a different loop` errors resolved at the test-fixture layer so that the suite errors drop without changing production database behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The fixture/conftest change does not modify api/database.py production code paths (test-infra only)
`python3 -m pytest api/tests -q` reports 0 errors attributable to event-loop/Future-loop binding
Error count strictly decreases vs the US-001 baseline; no previously-passing test regresses
evidence/launch-readiness/US-002-event-loop-fix.md captures before/after pytest tail

userStories.title: Update legacy auth test expectations to 410 post-Auth0
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want legacy auth tests that expect 200 updated to assert 410 (Gone) so that the suite reflects the post-Auth0-migration contract without altering any endpoint behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Only test files change — no api/routers/auth.py or api/ production source edits
Each updated test references the ADR/migration that made the endpoint return 410
`python3 -m pytest api/tests -q -k auth` shows the previously-failing legacy-auth tests now pass
evidence/launch-readiness/US-003-legacy-auth-410.md lists each test flipped 200->410

userStories.title: Diagnose /v1/report uniqueidentifier 500 (protected fix)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the `/v1/report` SQL uniqueidentifier-conversion 500 root-caused with a proposed minimal fix so the owner can approve a change that touches database behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/launch-readiness/US-004-report-500-diagnosis.md identifies the exact conversion site and failing query
A minimal proposed patch is included as a diff in the evidence file (NOT applied) pending owner approval
TDD anchor named: the pytest test id that flips red->green once the fix lands
Owner approval recorded before any api/ production code is modified

userStories.title: Repair public signup CTA (404 -> working signup)
userStories.description: As a prospective customer, I want the public signup CTA to reach a working signup flow so I can create an account. Fix changes the auth entry flow and is owner-gated.
acceptanceCriteria: Owner decision recorded: route /signup to Auth0 `?screen_hint=signup` (302) OR ship a signup page (200)
`curl -sS -I https://app.sigilsec.ai/signup` returns 200 or an intentional 302 to the Auth0 signup screen (not 404)
evidence/launch-readiness/US-005-signup-cta.md captures pre-fix 404 and post-fix curl
No auth-flow code merged without owner approval (probation gate)

userStories.title: Next.js upgrade assessment for high-severity advisory
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a written upgrade assessment for the breaking Next.js bump (current -> 16.2.7) so the operator can execute the forced upgrade with known blast radius.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/launch-readiness/US-006-nextjs-upgrade-assessment.md captures `cd dashboard && npm audit --audit-level=high --omit=dev` output and the advisory IDs
Breaking-change surface enumerated (Next.js major migration notes mapped to dashboard usage)
Rollback plan and test/build verification commands listed for the operator
Assessment does NOT run `npm audit fix --force` (operator-gated execution)

userStories.title: Execute Next.js upgrade and clear audit
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the planned Next.js upgrade applied so the production dependency audit is clean.
acceptanceCriteria: `cd dashboard && npm audit --audit-level=high --omit=dev` exits 0
`cd dashboard && npm test -- --runInBand` passes and `npm run build` compiles successfully
evidence/launch-readiness/US-007-nextjs-upgrade-applied.md captures post-upgrade audit + test + build output
Upgrade follows the US-006 assessment; deviations documented

userStories.title: Draft Rust CLI verification CI job
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a CI workflow that installs a pinned Rust toolchain and runs `cargo test` so the Rust CLI becomes verifiable without relying on an unconfigured local toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Actions workflow drafted that runs `rustup default <pinned>` then `cargo test` against cli/
Workflow YAML validates (`yamllint` or actionlint clean) and references the existing cli/Cargo.toml
evidence/launch-readiness/US-008-rust-ci.md explains why local `cargo --version` failed and how CI closes it
Workflow is drafted/committed but enabling on protected branches noted as operator action

userStories.title: Verify Rust CLI builds and tests pass
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want `cargo test` to pass for the Rust CLI so the CLI claims in the launch report are verified rather than unverifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: `cargo test` (CI or configured local toolchain) exits 0 for cli/
evidence/launch-readiness/US-009-rust-verification.md captures the toolchain version and full test output
Any failing tests are filed and triaged, not hidden

userStories.title: [cross-ref] Pricing page reconciliation
userStories.description: As a launch gate, I want the stale pricing page ($199 Team, 30-day trial) reconciled with the billing API ($99 Team). This work is already tracked under F-003 — F-007 only gates launch on its completion; do not duplicate the stories here.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser probe of https://www.sigilsec.ai/pricing shows Team $99 and trial copy matching the owner ADR-0001 decision
F-003 STORY-112 age header < 3600 on a fresh probe AND content reflects current main (operator fresh deploy)
evidence/launch-readiness/US-010-pricing-crossref.md links the closing F-003 evidence files
No new pricing stories created in F-007 (traceability stays in F-003)

userStories.title: [cross-ref] Installer URL serves real installer
userStories.description: As a launch gate, I want https://www.sigilsec.ai/install.sh to serve the real installer (GitHub main) instead of the private-development copy. Same CDN/fresh-deploy root cause as F-003 STORY-108/112; tracked under F-004 distribution ACs.
acceptanceCriteria: `curl -sS https://www.sigilsec.ai/install.sh | head -5` matches the GitHub main installer (`#!/usr/bin/env sh` / `# Sigil installer`), not the 'private development' copy
Root cause confirmed identical to the F-003 STORY-108/112 stale-deploy finding (or new cause documented)
evidence/launch-readiness/US-011-installer-crossref.md captures pre/post curl and links F-004 status
No new installer stories created in F-007 (traceability stays in F-004)

userStories.title: Re-run launch-readiness report and flip verdict
userStories.description: As the owner, I want the launch-readiness report re-run once all blockers clear so the verdict moves from NOT READY to READY with refreshed evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/launch-readiness-report.md re-run with current evidence; every CRITICAL/HIGH blocker shows resolved or owner-accepted
Verdict line reads READY (or NOT READY with an explicit, owner-acknowledged residual list)
SOLUTION.md F-007 status flipped to DONE with all feature-level ACs ticked and evidence paths inline
progress.md US-001..US-012 reflect final status
